# SOMspec — Semantic Object Model Specification

> SOMspec defines SOM (Semantic Object Model), an open JSON format for representing web pages for AI agent consumption. Achieves ~17× average token reduction versus raw HTML, with peaks above 100× on large pages.

## Key resources

- Specification: https://somspec.org/spec
- Reference (implementations): https://somspec.org/reference
- Validator: https://somspec.org/validate
- robots.txt directives proposal: https://somspec.org/directives
- Calculator (token economics): https://somspec.org/calculator
- Publisher leaderboard: https://somspec.org/publishers
- Compliance matrix (frameworks/agents): https://somspec.org/compliance
- Changelog: https://somspec.org/changelog
- Blog: https://somspec.org/blog
- GitHub: https://github.com/dbhurley/somspec
- Reference implementation: https://plasmate.app
- W3C Community Group: https://www.w3.org/community/web-content-browser-ai/

## About SOM

SOM replaces raw HTML as the format AI agents use to understand web pages.

- 17× fewer tokens than raw HTML on average (peak 118× measured)
- Typed element roles (link, button, heading, input, etc.)
- Stable SHA-256 element IDs across fetches
- Semantic region detection (main, nav, aside, header, footer)
- Publisher-servable as alternate representation via SOM Directives in robots.txt
